page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd is a general-purpose, storage-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 44 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 176 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is a key highlight, featuring 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity distributed across 8 local drives, while no dedicated GPU hardware is included.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU encryption score of 38,720.91 and a static web serving throughput of 40.85 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Image processing performance is also strong, scoring 16,162.0 on Geekbench. In contrast, standard compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core (36,641.54) and uncached memory read bandwidth (111.44 GB/sec), fall within the average range. For machine learning tasks, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support enables strong prompt processing speeds of 175.16 tokens/sec on medium 7B LLM models, though text generation on large 70B models is limited to a poor tier of 2.37 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-44-lssd offers high resource density by bundling substantial local NVMe SSD storage directly with dedicated compute resources, reducing the need for external block storage attachments for high-I/O operations. This hardware profile is highly suited for high-throughput databases, static web serving, intermediate data processing pipelines, and localized software compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd is a general-purpose instance featuring 44 dedicated vCPUs (22 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 176 GB of RAM and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives, with no GPU acceleration. The instance demonstrates strong performance in static web serving (40.85 GB/sec), CPU encryption, and image processing, while maintaining average scores in general multi-core compute and memory bandwidth. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supports efficient low-parameter LLM inference, though performance drops significantly on larger 70B models. This configuration provides a cost-effective balance of compute and high-speed local storage, making it highly suitable for database hosting, web serving, and data-intensive workloads requiring low-latency local I/O.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd is a general-purpose instance with 44 vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd features 44 vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring forty-four dedicated vCPUs and over three terabytes of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz
176 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives
Strong performance in static web serving and CPU encryption tasks
Intel AMX support for accelerated low-parameter LLM inference
Best suited for high-I/O databases, web serving, and data processing
